title: B4/B8 R161 source-faithful VF2 score audit

source_fold.node_order: first-seen virtual qubits from two-qubit input operations
source_fold.edge_order: first-seen unique directed virtual interaction edges
source_fold.term_order: all interaction nodes, then all interaction edges
source_fold.combination: binary64 multiply by interaction count, then left-to-right binary64 addition
source_fold.restriction: strictly less than the current Decreasing upper bound

oracle.score_domain: Fraction.from_float of each source-transformed binary64 term
oracle.feasibility: a mapping is feasible only when every required node and directed edge has an ErrorMap value or reversed directed fallback
oracle.tie_rule: all mappings with exactly equal minimum rational score are minimizers

acceptance_conditions.label: R160 result and all sixteen worker payloads validate
the source transform and source-lineage binding are explicit
all 5040 mappings are scored for every profile/case
the source-faithful rational oracle and source-order binary64 fold are both retained
every R160 selected vector is classified against both score models
all R160 exact-oracle failures are retained without relabeling
the result distinguishes source-f64 consistency from exact-rational correctness
no source patch, hardware execution, simulation, shots, hidden selection, or new route is used
no compiler-bug, quantum-advantage, BQP, solved-frontier, or new-credit claim is made

claim_boundary.what_is_supported: a reproducible diagnostic of whether R160's rejected rows remain rejected under the source-faithful neg-log-fidelity score and whether the observed mapping is consistent with the source-order binary64 fold
claim_boundary.what_is_not_supported: a source fix, a causal compiler explanation, a general numerical theorem, cross-platform determinism, hardware relevance, route advantage, quantum advantage, BQP separation, solved B4/B8/B10, or new credit
